name: Barbecued Lima Beans (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, allowing extra for folding over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe.
2. Add beans to boiling water and return to boil. Boil beans 2 minutes, then soak beans for 1 hour (or overnight if preferred).
3. Cook beans slowly until tender, about 45 minutes. Drain and save 2 cups cooking liquid.
4. Brown salt pork with onion. Mix remaining ingredients with the salt pork mixture. Add beans and stir gently. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each baking pan.
5. To serve without freezing: Preheat oven to 400°F (hot). Bake for 30 minutes.
6. To freeze: Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature, then complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food. Fold edges together and seal with freezer tape. Label with the name of the food, date frozen, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months). Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
7. To heat frozen food: Preheat oven to 400°F (hot). Remove freezer wrap. Place food in a baking pan and bake for 1 hour or until the center is hot.
